title: Inventory Delta Loader
prompt: Implement `load_inventory_deltas(lines: list[str]) -> dict[str, int]`. Each non-empty non-comment line is `sku,delta`. Strip whitespace around fields. Ignore blank lines, lines whose first non-space character is `#`, malformed lines, empty SKUs, and non-integer deltas. Sum deltas per SKU in first-seen key insertion order. Do not mutate the input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: User Intake Normalizer
prompt: Implement `normalize_user_record(record: dict) -> dict`. Return a new dict with keys `email`, `display_name`, and `active`. Email is `record['email']` stripped and lowercased, or an empty string if missing. Display name is stripped `record['display_name']`; if missing or blank, use the email local-part before `@`; if that is blank, use `unknown`. Active is false only when `record['active']` is one of `False`, `0`, `'false'`, `'no'`, or `'0'`; compare strings case-insensitively after stripping. Do not mutate the input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Ticket Router
prompt: Implement `route_ticket(ticket: dict) -> str`. Return `security` if any label equals `security` case-insensitively or the title contains `breach`, `token`, or `secret` case-insensitively. Return `payments` if the component is `billing`, `invoice`, or `checkout` case-insensitively. Return `urgent-support` if priority is `p0` or `p1` case-insensitively. Otherwise return `support`. Missing fields count as empty. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Feature Flag Reader
prompt: Implement `read_feature_flags(env: dict[str, str], prefix: str = 'FEATURE_') -> dict[str, bool]`. For keys starting with prefix, create an output key by removing the prefix, lowercasing, and replacing double underscores with dots. Values `1`, `true`, `yes`, and `on` are true; values `0`, `false`, `no`, and `off` are false; compare after stripping and lowercasing. Ignore any other value. Preserve insertion order from the input mapping. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Release Note Grouper
prompt: Implement `group_release_notes(commits: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[str]]`. Each commit may have `type`, `scope`, and `summary`. Return keys `feature`, `fix`, `docs`, and `other` in that order. Type `feat` maps to `feature`; `fix` maps to `fix`; `docs` maps to `docs`; anything else maps to `other`. Each entry is `scope: summary` when scope is non-empty after stripping, otherwise just summary. Strip all strings. Skip commits with a blank summary. Do not mutate input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Secret Redactor
prompt: Implement `redact_secrets(config: dict) -> dict`. Return a deep-copied structure where dictionary values are replaced with `'***'` when the key contains `password`, `secret`, `token`, or `api_key` case-insensitively. Recurse through dictionaries and lists. Leave all other values unchanged. Do not mutate the input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: SLA Breach Summary
prompt: Implement `summarize_sla_breaches(tickets: list[dict], limit_hours: int) -> dict[str, int]`. Count tickets whose `status` is not `closed` case-insensitively and whose `age_hours` is greater than limit_hours. Group counts by `team`, using `unassigned` for missing or blank teams after stripping. Return groups in first-seen insertion order. Ignore tickets with missing or non-numeric age_hours. Do not mutate the input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Slug Allocator
prompt: Implement `allocate_slug(title: str, existing: list[str]) -> str`. Normalize the title by lowercasing ASCII letters and digits, replacing each run of non-alphanumeric characters with one hyphen, and trimming leading/trailing hyphens. Use `item` if the normalized title is empty. If the slug already exists in existing, append `-2`, then `-3`, and so on until unused. Existing comparison is case-sensitive after normalization only for the new title. Do not mutate existing. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Retry Policy Parser
prompt: Implement `parse_retry_policy(policy: str) -> dict[str, int]`. Parse semicolon-separated `key=value` pairs. Recognized keys are `attempts`, `backoff`, and `jitter`. Strip whitespace. Values must be non-negative base-10 integers. Defaults are attempts=1, backoff=0, jitter=0. Ignore unknown keys, malformed pairs, blank keys, and invalid values. Later recognized valid values override earlier ones. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Invoice Rollup
prompt: Implement `invoice_rollup(items: list[dict], tax_rate: float) -> dict[str, int]`. Each item has `quantity` and `unit_cents`; missing values default to zero. Ignore items with quantity <= 0 or unit_cents < 0. Return `subtotal_cents`, `tax_cents`, and `total_cents`. Tax is `round(subtotal_cents * tax_rate)` using Python's normal round behavior. Do not mutate the input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Order Payment Reconciliation
prompt: Implement `reconcile_orders(orders: list[dict], payments: list[dict]) -> list[dict]`. Each order has `id` and `total_cents`. Each payment has `order_id`, `amount_cents`, and optional `status`. Count only payments whose status is missing or `captured` case-insensitively. For each order in input order, return `{id, paid_cents, balance_cents, status}`. Status is `paid` when paid equals total, `partial` when paid is positive but below total, `overpaid` when paid exceeds total, otherwise `unpaid`. Missing numeric values count as zero. Do not mutate inputs. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Workflow Definition Validator
prompt: Implement `validate_workflow(definition: dict) -> list[str]`. The definition has `steps`, a list of dicts with `id` and optional `needs` list. Return sorted unique error strings. Add `duplicate step: X` for repeated non-empty IDs. Add `missing dependency: A -> B` when step A needs B and B is not a defined ID. Add `self dependency: X` when a step needs itself. Add `cycle detected` once if dependencies contain any cycle among defined IDs. Ignore blank step IDs for graph purposes. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: JSON Patch Lite
prompt: Implement `apply_patch_ops(document: dict, ops: list[dict]) -> dict`. Return a deep-copied document after applying supported operations in order. Paths are slash-separated object keys like `/profile/name`; no array indexing is required. Support `set` with `value`, creating intermediate dicts as needed, and `remove`, which deletes an existing key and does nothing when absent. Ignore operations with missing path, path not starting with `/`, empty final key, or unsupported op. Do not mutate inputs. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Inventory Allocation
prompt: Implement `allocate_inventory(stock: dict[str, int], requests: list[dict]) -> list[dict]`. Process requests in input order. Each request has `id`, `sku`, and `quantity`. Quantity <= 0 is rejected. Allocate up to available stock for that SKU. Return one result per request with `id`, `sku`, `requested`, `allocated`, and `status`. Status is `filled`, `partial`, `rejected`, or `backordered`. Backordered means requested positive quantity and allocated zero. Do not mutate stock or requests. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Deployment Plan Order
prompt: Implement `deployment_order(services: list[str], dependencies: list[list[str]]) -> list[str]`. Each dependency `[before, after]` means before must deploy before after. Return the lexicographically smallest valid order containing every distinct service from services. Ignore dependencies mentioning unknown services. Duplicate dependencies may occur. Return `[]` if a cycle exists. Do not mutate inputs. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Profile Merge
prompt: Implement `merge_profiles(primary: dict, secondary: dict) -> dict`. Return a new merged profile. For scalar fields, keep primary's value unless it is missing, None, or a blank string after stripping. For `emails`, combine primary then secondary emails, stripping and lowercasing, removing blanks and duplicates in first-seen order. For `metadata`, shallow-merge dictionaries with secondary first and primary overriding. Do not mutate inputs. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Incident Timeline
prompt: Implement `incident_timeline(events: list[dict]) -> list[dict]`. Each event has `time`, `service`, `status`, and optional `message`. Sort by time ascending, preserving input order for equal times. Collapse consecutive events for the same service and status into one output item with `time` from the first event and `count`. Include `service`, `status`, `time`, `count`, and `messages`, where messages are non-blank stripped messages in original collapsed order. Do not mutate input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Cache Replay
prompt: Implement `replay_cache_events(events: list[dict], capacity: int) -> list[str]`. Simulate an LRU cache of string keys. Supported ops are `get` and `put`. `get` for an existing key marks it most recently used. `put` inserts or updates and marks most recently used. When capacity is exceeded, evict least recently used. Capacity <= 0 means the cache stays empty. Return final keys from least to most recently used. Ignore events missing a key or with unsupported op. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: API Error Compressor
prompt: Implement `compress_api_errors(events: list[dict]) -> list[dict]`. Group adjacent events with the same `endpoint` and integer `status` when status >= 400. Ignore events with status < 400 or non-integer status. For each group return `endpoint`, `status`, `count`, `first_time`, and `last_time`. Missing endpoint becomes `unknown`; missing time becomes empty string. Do not mutate input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.

title: Webhook Payload Transformer
prompt: Implement `transform_webhook_payload(payload: dict) -> dict`. Return a new dict with `event_id`, `event_type`, `actor_email`, and `resources`. `event_id` is string value of `id` or empty string. `event_type` is lowercased string value of `type` with spaces replaced by underscores. `actor_email` is lowercased stripped `actor.email`, or empty string if absent. `resources` is a list of `{kind, id}` from payload `resources`, keeping only items with non-blank kind and id after stripping; cast both to strings. Do not mutate input. Return only Python code defining the function, with no markdown.
